lean_stdlib_notes.no_by_contra: by_contra is 'unknown tactic' in Lean 4.28.0 - use Nat.le_of_not_lt instead
lean_stdlib_notes.no_push_neg: push_neg not available without Mathlib - use Nat.not_le.mp or omega
lean_stdlib_notes.no_pairwise_iff_get: List.pairwise_iff_get does not exist - use List.pairwise_cons + List.get_mem
lean_stdlib_notes.list_mem: List.mem_cons_self has NO extra args (implicit). List.get_mem xs Fin for membership.
lean_stdlib_notes.import_placement: imports MUST be at the very top of the file, before any doc comments or code
lean_stdlib_notes.theorem_ordering: Define theorems BEFORE use - cannot forward-reference.
lean_stdlib_notes.barrel_file: formal-verification/lean/FVSquad.lean must import ALL module files for lake build to compile them
lean_stdlib_notes.doc_comments_before_eval: Use regular -- comments before #eval. Doc comments /-- -/ before #eval cause parse errors.
lean_stdlib_notes.open_namespace: MajorityVote.lean has no namespace - use majority etc. directly after import, no open needed.
lean_stdlib_notes.mergeSort_perm: List.mergeSort_perm l (fun a b => decide (a >= b)) (must be explicit)
lean_stdlib_notes.filter_sublist: Use by_cases + List.Sublist.cons2/cons for filter monotone proofs
lean_stdlib_notes.getD_in_bounds: l.getD i 0 = l.get {i, h} - use induction + simp [List.getD_eq_getElem?_getD]
lean_stdlib_notes.pairwise_get_antitone: Prove by induction on l: zero case uses pairwise_cons + get_mem, succ case uses get_cons_succ
lean_stdlib_notes.sortDesc_get_antitone: i <= j -> sortDesc(l)[j] <= sortDesc(l)[i]: split eq/lt, use pairwise_ge_antitone for strict
